task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Unbalanced_Load_Alert' alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Alerts' page, a success modal, or the 'Save As Alert' dialog showing the exact name 'Unbalanced_Load_Alert'.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert is scheduled on a recurring basis and has a trigger condition for >40% traffic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the alert configuration screen showing a recurring 'Schedule' (e.g., 'Run on Cron Schedule' or 'Run every hour') and 'Trigger Conditions' that evaluate if a host exceeds 40% traffic (e.g., a custom condition or SPL search string checking for > 40).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Web_Infrastructure_Health' dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Dashboards list or the active dashboard view displaying the exact title 'Web_Infrastructure_Health'.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains a 'Traffic Distribution' panel showing event counts or percentages by host.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dashboard panel titled 'Traffic Distribution'. It must contain a visualization (such as a pie chart, bar chart, or data table) that groups data by 'host' and displays either event counts or traffic percentages.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains a 'Server Error Counts' panel showing HTTP errors (status >= 400) grouped by host.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dashboard panel titled 'Server Error Counts'. It must contain a visualization grouping data by 'host' and showing counts of HTTP errors (often visible via a search string containing 'status>=400' or status codes like 404, 500, etc. in the table/chart).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Splunk Web UI to create the artifacts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Splunk Enterprise web interface (typically at localhost:8000) being actively navigated, rather than just terminal commands or text editors modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Ensure the dashboard panels are rendering real Splunk search results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for authentic Splunk UI elements like loading bars, search job indicators, or actual rendered charts/tables in the dashboard panels, ensuring the agent didn't just type static text into a markdown panel.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the dashboard or alert names.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the browser developer tools (F12/Inspect) are not open and being used to modify the DOM text to 'Unbalanced_Load_Alert' or 'Web_Infrastructure_Health'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Index: 'tutorial'. Alert Name: 'Unbalanced_Load_Alert'. Dashboard Name: 'Web_Infrastructure_Health'. Splunk Credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!
